content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Maxine Waters 
California state Assembly 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
May 10, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-261 
Dear Assemblywoman Waters: 
You have requested advice concerning your responsibilities 
under the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act. 1/ 
This letter confirms that your letter dated April 20, 1989, 
accurately summar.izes the verbal advice I provided you on 
April 19th regarding mailing of announcements to your constituents 
of the Cinco de Mayo event cosponsored by you and the Employment 
Development Department. 
A copy of your incoming letter is 
enclosed for your convenience. 
Please contact me at 322-5901 if you have any further 
questions regarding this matter. 
KED: LS:ld 
Sincerely, 
By.: 
COunsel, 
E. Donovan 
Counsel 
S i* . 
gal~ViSion 


sstmb1tt 
tgislaturt 
WATERS 
CAUCUS CHAIRWOMAN 
April 20, 1989 
Ms. Lilly spitz 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Lilly: 
COMMITTEES 
GOVERNMENTAL ORGANiZATION 
This letter is to confirm our conversation of yesterday 
regarding the application of the mass mailing prohibitions of the 
political reform act to an official agency event being sponsored 
by the Employment Development Department and myself. 
We are planning a Cinco de Mayo official agency event for the 
purpose of informing my constituents of the government services 
available to them on the state and local level. 
I understand that 
regulation 18901 (f) (10) allows us to mail announcements of the 
Cinco de Mayo official agency event to my constituents without 
limitation, so long as the notice specifies only the time, date, 
place, and description of the subject matter of the meeting. 
My 
name may appear once on this notice. 
If this is in accord with your memory of our conversation, I 
would appreciate confirmation of this letter, in writing as soon 
as possible. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
MAXINE WATERS 
MW/RSC/rj 
ASSEMBLYWOMAN, 48TH DISTRICT 


Ms. Lilly spitz
